sanskrit:
स्वधर्ममपि चावेक्ष्य न विकम्पितुमर्हसि।
धर्म्याद्धि युद्धाच्छ्रेयोऽन्यत्क्षत्रियस्य न विद्यते॥
transliteration: sva-dharmam api cāvekṣya na vikampitum arhasi | dharmyād dhi yuddhāc chreyo ’nyat kṣatriyasya na vidyate ||
literal_meaning: Having considered your own duty, you ought not to waver; for a kṣatriya there is no higher good than a righteous battle.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reframes Arjuna’s existential paralysis as a violation of his core identity: the warrior’s ‘righteous battle’ is not optional violence but the indispensable ritual through which his psyche maintains coherence and society preserves order.
psychological_state: Moral injury—Arjuna experiences the collision between his idealized self-image (non-violent sage) and his actual social identity (protector-warrior), producing ego-dystonic anxiety.
trigger_scenario: A surgeon with PTSD is told, ‘Your duty is to cut; if you refuse, more will die.’ The ethical scalpel becomes both savior and executioner.
anchor_text: Stand firm in your duty, even when the path is painful.
